explanation: Bypasses the 'Net.WebClient' detection by dynamically concatenating the class name string at runtime.
Utilizes the System.Net.Http.HttpClient class, which achieves the same web request functionality but is entirely omitted from the rule's selection criteria.
Bypasses the 'iwr ' detection by removing the trailing space. PowerShell allows evaluating the URI directly within parentheses, evading the exact substring match.
Employs PowerShell backtick obfuscation. The command executes normally, but the process command-line logs will register the backticks, bypassing the 'Invoke-WebRequest' string match.
Leverages the Msxml2.XMLHTTP COM object to initiate a web request, a method not monitored by the provided Sigma rule.
